methods.description: Evaluation of the analytical performance characteristics of the developed method (likely for BZD analysis in urine) under optimal conditions, including linearity, correlation coefficients, limit of detection (LOD), limit of quantification (LOQ), accuracy, precision, enrichment factors (EFs), and reproducibility.

steps.description: The standard calibration curves were constructed with standard solutions at 2, 10, 25, 50, 100, 250 and 500 ng/mL (n = 3).
steps.searchable_summary: Generate standard calibration curves for analytical method validation
inputs: Analytical measurements (e.g., peak area) for each standard solution replicate (n=3)

steps.description: Determine the linearity range and correlation coefficients (r) from the standard calibration curves.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate linearity range and correlation coefficient (r) from calibration curve data

steps.description: Determine the LODs and LOQs of all analytes in spiked urine samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate limit of detection (LOD) and limit of quantification (LOQ) for analytes in spiked matrix samples
inputs: Analytical measurements from spiked urine samples (low concentrations or blank + noise)
Statistical method for calculation (e.g., S/N ratio, standard deviation of blank)

steps.description: Assess the accuracy of this method using the recoveries of BZDs at 5, 10 and 20 ng/mL concentrations in blank urine samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate recovery percentages to assess method accuracy in spiked matrix samples
inputs: Analytical measurements (e.g., peak area or concentration)
outputs: Recovery percentages for each concentration level

steps.description: Calculate the average recoveries of three BZDs.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate average recovery percentages from accuracy assessment data

steps.description: Assess the intra-day and inter-day precisions (relative standard deviations, RSDs) for three replicate extractions of BZDs.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative standard deviations (RSDs) for replicate analyses to assess intra-day and inter-day precision
inputs: Analytical measurements (e.g., concentrations) from replicate extractions performed within a day (intra-day) and on different days (inter-day)

steps.description: Determine the EFs when the loading volume of the diluted urine sample (urine/H 2 O, 3/1, v/v) was fixed at 8 mL.
inputs: Analytical measurements from extracted samples (8 mL diluted urine loaded)
Analytical measurements from direct analysis of the same concentration

steps.description: Prepare the diluted urine sample (urine/H 2 O, 3/1, v/v) (8 mL) spiked with 20 ng/mL of each analyte for reproducibility testing.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare spiked diluted urine sample for reproducibility testing
outputs: Spiked diluted urine sample (8 mL, 20 ng/mL)

steps.description: The diluted urine sample (urine/H 2 O, 3/1, v/v) (8 mL) spiked with 20 ng/mL of each analyte was loaded onto ZIF-8@Dt-COOH(2×) cartridges...
steps.searchable_summary: Load spiked diluted urine sample onto analytical cartridges for reproducibility testing
inputs: Spiked diluted urine sample (8 mL, 20 ng/mL)
ZIF-8@Dt-COOH(2×) cartridges (multiple from same batch and different batches)

steps.description: Calculate the RSDs of the recoveries of BZDs for column-to-column precision using three cartridges from one batch.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative standard deviations (RSDs) of recoveries for multiple analytical columns from the same batch to assess column-to-column reproducibility
inputs: Recoveries obtained from analyzing spiked diluted urine loaded onto three cartridges from one batch

steps.description: Calculate the RSDs of the recoveries of BZDs for batch-to-batch precision using three parallel batches.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate relative standard deviations (RSDs) of recoveries for multiple batches of analytical columns to assess batch-to-batch reproducibility
inputs: Recoveries obtained from analyzing spiked diluted urine loaded onto cartridges from three parallel batches

steps.description: Obtain representative HPLC chromatograms of the standard solution (300 ng/mL of each analyte) and urine spiked with 10 ng/mL of each analyte via direct analysis, extracted by ZIF-8@Dt-COOH(2×) and commercial CX adsorbents.
steps.searchable_summary: Generate HPLC chromatogram plots for different sample types

steps.description: Compare the HPLC chromatograms to assess enrichment and impurity reduction.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare HPLC chromatograms to evaluate enrichment and reduction of impurities
outputs: Qualitative assessment of enrichment and impurity reduction

steps.description: Evaluate the recovery (%) by the following equation:
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate recovery percentage using a specified equation
inputs: Analytical measurements (e.g., peak areas or concentrations) from spiked and unspiked samples
Specified recovery equation (not provided in text)
